authoritative_sources.title: 29 CFR Part 1926 - Safety and Health Regulations for Construction
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The current eCFR text is the primary federal construction safety regulation and is authoritative for hazard controls, training, and construction-site compliance obligations.
used_for: refusal boundary when work would proceed without required protections

authoritative_sources.title: 29 CFR Part 1904 - Recording and Reporting Occupational Injuries and Illnesses
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The current eCFR text is the primary federal rule for OSHA injury and illness reporting and recordkeeping evidence used in construction incident and compliance review.
used_for: refusal when recordkeeping gaps change the answer

authoritative_sources.title: Recommended Practices for Safety and Health Programs in Construction
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OSHA's construction-specific guidance is authoritative for translating the regulatory baseline into site-specific hazard identification, training, worker participation, evaluation, and multi-employer coordination workflow.

authoritative_sources.title: Multi-Employer Citation Policy
authoritative_sources.source_class: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OSHA's directive is the primary public enforcement guidance for determining safety responsibility boundaries among controlling, correcting, creating, and exposing employers on multi-employer jobsites.
used_for: subcontractor coordination and sequencing boundary handling
handoff when safety authority is disputed

authoritative_sources.title: Field Operations Manual - Chapter 5
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OSHA's Field Operations Manual is the authoritative enforcement manual for how inspection records, photographs, notes, and abatement documentation are assembled and handled.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Inspection evidence capture and review readiness

authoritative_sources.title: Work Permits Guide
authoritative_sources.publisher: New York City Department of Buildings
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This is an official public authority guide showing a real permit workflow from application through inspections, final sign-off, and certificate issuance, making it authoritative for jurisdictional gate structure even though local sequences vary.

authoritative_sources.title: Project Management Guide: Design/Build-Bridging Project Delivery Methods
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GSA's public construction project management guide is authoritative for substantial completion, final cleaning, financial closeout, and project-management closeout workflow on public construction projects.
used_for: review packet design for completion blockers

authoritative_sources.title: EM 385-1-1 Safety and Occupational Health Requirements
authoritative_sources.source_class: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: USACE EM 385-1-1 is a recognized public construction operating standard for contractor safety programs, activity hazard analyses, subcontractor controls, maintenance and service work, and hierarchy when stricter requirements apply.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Site-specific execution and service-work control handling

workflow_stages.name: Request intake and jurisdiction-aware boundary triage
workflow_stages.description: Identify site type, jurisdiction, permit posture, trade scope, and whether the request is supportable inside a construction safety-compliance lane before making recommendations.
source_classes: Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance
Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards

workflow_stages.name: Obligation and control mapping
workflow_stages.description: Map construction safety, recordkeeping, permit, and closeout obligations to the exact controls, owners, and evidence required for the named site or work order.
source_classes: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards

workflow_stages.name: Evidence assembly and review readiness
workflow_stages.description: Collect and validate permits, inspections, hazard analyses, training, observations, subcontractor records, photos, and corrective actions needed to support a compliance summary or escalation packet.
source_classes: Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance

workflow_stages.name: Site progression, inspection, and closeout gating
workflow_stages.description: Determine whether the site can mobilize, continue, or close based on permit state, changed conditions, failed inspections, substantial completion posture, and final-signoff dependencies.
source_classes: Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance

workflow_stages.name: Exception, ambiguity, and changed-condition escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate when policy, jurisdiction, safety conditions, or owner responsibility conflicts materially change the answer or move authority outside the lane.
source_classes: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards

workflow_stages.name: Review and attestation preparation
workflow_stages.description: Prepare evidence-linked summaries and blocker notes for downstream reviewers without implying final permit approval, code interpretation, or licensed signoff.
source_classes: Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance

artifacts: inspection requests, reports, and deficiency notices
site logs, pretask plans, JHAs, and AHAs
OSHA recordkeeping and incident logs when applicable
subcontractor rosters, orientation records, and sequencing notes
change orders, closeout packages, final cleaning evidence, and substantial-completion records
field photos, installation evidence, and corrective-action trackers

allowed: Classify requests as in-scope, out-of-scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites for construction and field-services safety compliance.
Produce evidence-backed control matrices, evidence-gap summaries, exception notes, and review packets tied to named site and work-order records.
Map permit, inspection, closeout, subcontractor, and changed-condition dependencies to the correct owner without improvising code or licensed-trade authority.
escalate: Required tenant facts, site records, permit context, or jurisdiction instructions are missing and materially change the outcome.
Public-source, jurisdictional, and tenant-policy conflicts materially change whether work can proceed or close out.
Changed site conditions, failed inspections, multi-employer responsibility disputes, or safety incidents move authority to safety leadership, permitting, inspection, legal, or licensed trade owners.
refuse: Requests for legal interpretation, regulator negotiation, final signoff, permit approval, code interpretation, or licensed trade or engineering judgment.
Requests to proceed, certify, or close out work when required safety, permit, inspection, or evidence gates are incomplete or disputed.
Requests that would bypass documented site-specific hazard analysis, training, recordkeeping, or inspection dependencies.

domain_failure_modes: Treating a permit or inspection sequence from one jurisdiction as universal and giving confident advice without confirming the actual authority having jurisdiction.
Allowing work to proceed or close out even though the site-specific hazard analysis, training, permit posting, or inspection evidence is missing or stale.
Collapsing changed site conditions or a near miss into a minor note instead of a stop-and-refresh condition that changes the answer.
Conflating construction coordination with licensed trade judgment, code interpretation, or engineer-of-record authority.
Ignoring multi-employer control realities and assigning responsibility without preserving who controls sequencing, subcontractors, or correction authority.
Treating substantial completion or final cleaning as equivalent to final inspection acceptance or regulator signoff.
Producing generic compliance advice that ignores work-order, site-condition, customer-site, or closeout dependencies specific to field service and construction work.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Request that implies licensed trade or code-interpretation authority
Missing evidence for a required control or obligation
Request for legal interpretation, regulator negotiation, or formal signoff
A required control is missing evidence
Normal in-scope execution using realistic industry terminology and artifact shapes
Boundary rejection when the request should move to an adjacent specialist
Low-confidence handling when required facts, records, or authority sources are missing
Conflicting-source or conflicting-policy handling that requires escalation instead of confident synthesis
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline and records assumptions

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction-specific permit and inspection sequencing differences
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Public permitting workflows vary materially by authority having jurisdiction, so the pack records the need for tenant-supplied jurisdiction context and refuses case-specific sequencing when that context is missing.

notes: The source set intentionally mixes primary OSHA rules, OSHA enforcement guidance, public-owner closeout workflow, and a jurisdictional permit workflow example so the pack covers obligations, evidence, terminology, and failure handling rather than only one dimension.
The pack treats local permit and inspection rules as jurisdiction-sensitive by design and escalates when they materially change the answer.
